claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children are you responsible for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking into claiming Child Benefit. I have three children - Alex who is 7, Blake who is 20, and Charlie who is also 7.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have three children: Alex (7), Blake (20), and Charlie (7).

I need to ask you a few questions to determine your eligibility for each child.

**First, let me ask: Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's an important requirement.

Now, let me ask about your living situation with your children:

**Do you live with Alex, Blake, and Charlie, or do any of them live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They all live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to understand a bit more about Blake's situation, since at age 20, the rules are different:

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training (such as university, college, or an apprenticeship)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment. He actually works full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

**Does Blake work 24 hours or more per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he does work 24 hours or more per week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's important for Blake's eligibility.

Now, for Alex and Charlie, I have one more question:

**Are you the only person claiming Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, or is someone else also claiming for them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm the only one claiming for them - nobody else is getting Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me now provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Eligibility

**You are eligible for Child Benefit for:**
- ✅ **Alex** (age 7)
- ✅ **Charlie** (age 7)

**You are NOT eligible for Child Benefit for:**
- ❌ **Blake** (age 20)

### Explanation

Alex and Charlie both meet the eligibility criteria because they are under 16, live with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them.

Blake, however, is no longer eligible. Although Blake lives with you, the rules state that Child Benefit stops when a child starts paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Blake works full-time and is not in education or training, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex and meet the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Alex qualifies.

Blake is 20 years old and works 24 hours or more per week. According to the rules, Child Benefit stops if a child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in approved education or training and works full-time, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Charlie and meet the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Charlie qualifies.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, as both are under 16, live with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. However, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake because Blake is 20 years old, working 24 hours or more per week, and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit ceases in these circumstances.

Blake is 20 years old and works 24 hours or more per week. According to the rules, Child Benefit stops if a child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in approved education or training and works full-time, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Charlie and meet the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Charlie qualifies.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, as both are under 16, live with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. However, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake because Blake is 20 years old, working 24 hours or more per week, and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit ceases in these circumstances.
